paper_title: Experimental Analysis and Particle Size Analysis of Sediments in the Milan River Basin

methods.description: Process for measuring the particle size of sediment samples using a laser particle size analyzer to determine the distribution and classification of particles.

steps.description: Place about 0.8 g of sample in the beaker, add 10 mL of 10% H2O2, heat the mixture on the hot plate until the reaction is complete.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare sediment sample by reacting with H2O2 and heating

steps.description: Add 10 mL of 10% HCl and 10 mL of water, let the mixture stand for 12 h, wash the mixture with water.
steps.searchable_summary: Neutralize and wash sediment sample with HCl and water

steps.description: Add 10 mL of about 0.5 M sodium [NaPO3]6 as a dispersing agent. After the resulting mixture was shaken, it was analyzed on the machine.
steps.searchable_summary: Disperse sediment sample with sodium hexametaphosphate and analyze

steps.description: After the analysis was completed, the laser particle size analyzer automatically reported the index of each fraction of the sample, the median diameter, the average particle diameter, and the frequency curve.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze particle size distribution using laser particle size analyzer

methods.description: Procedure for determining the total nitrogen and organic carbon content in sediment samples using elemental analyzers.

steps.description: The vacuum freeze-dried samples were ground to a powder size of about 80 mesh, packaged in a tin capsule, and sent directly to an EA 3000 elemental analyzer for total nitrogen (TN) measurement.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare sediment sample for TN measurement by grinding and packaging

steps.description: An appropriate amount of lyophilized sample was placed into a standard silver cup, 2 drops of 5% HCl were added, the mixture was placed on a 60°C hot plate for 2 h, 1-2 drops of HCl were added, the hot plate was adjusted to 40ºC, and the sample was heated until it evaporated.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare sediment sample for organic carbon analysis by acid treatment and heating

steps.description: After this, the tin capsule was packaged, and it was analyzed using an EA 3000 elemental analyzer to determine the organic carbon.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze organic carbon content using elemental analyzer

methods.description: Procedure for determining the δ13C isotope content in sediment samples using an isotope mass spectrometer.

steps.description: A certain amount of lyophilized ground sediment sample was added to 10% dilute hydrochloric acid to remove the carbonate in the sample, and then, the sample was repeatedly washed with deionized water, air dried, and ground to less than 80 mesh.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove carbonates from sediment sample using HCl and prepare for isotope analysis

steps.description: A certain amount of sample was weighed and packed in a tin capsule. After burning the sample in a Flash 2000 organic elemental analyzer, the gas was sent to a Thermo Scientific isotope mass spectrometer to determine the δ13C.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine δ13C content using isotope mass spectrometer
